language.label: Language
language.description: Choose the language for the app interface.

hero.headline: Flashcards that work in Anki
hero.subtitle: Drop a Notion page and get a deck you don't have to fix — proper cloze, atomic cards, the right note types. PDF, Quizlet, Markdown, HTML, and CSV too.
hero.aiBadgeIntro: Clean cards, ready to study — proper cloze, atomic front and back, no empty backs. Prefer AI drafts?

notionToAnki.label: Notion to Anki
notionToAnki.body: Convert a Notion page into a deck — toggles become cards.

ankiToNotion.label: Anki to Notion
ankiToNotion.body: Turn an .apkg back into a Notion page you can keep editing.

imageOcclusion.label: Image occlusion
imageOcclusion.body: Mask labels on diagrams to make cards.

photoToDeck.label: Photo to deck
photoToDeck.body: Snap a page, get a deck.

mindMaps.label: Mind maps
mindMaps.body: Turn a mind map into structured cards.

noteTypes.label: Note types
noteTypes.body: Pick how the front and back of your cards look.

chat.label: Chat
chat.body: Turn your own notes into cards with AI.

print.label: Print
print.body: Print your deck on paper for offline study.

autoSync.label: Auto Sync
autoSync.body: Keep your Anki deck in sync with your Notion page.

walkthroughs.heading: Walkthroughs
walkthroughs.ctaBody: Made a video about 2anki? Contact us and we will feature it here for free.

dropzone.csvHint: Name your columns front and back (or question and answer) so 2anki maps them right — otherwise it uses the first column as the front and the second as the back.

form.fallbackDownload: Didn't get the file? Download it here.
form.linksExpire: Links expire in 2 hours — download now.
form.noCardsTitle: No cards found in this file
form.noCardsBody: No cards were found in this file. Most files need a toggle-list (Notion) or a question/answer pair to become cards.
form.imagesBody: No text to read, so there was nothing to turn into cards. Photo to Deck reads images with AI and drafts cards you review before download.
form.dropboxPrompt: Pick a file from your Dropbox to convert it into a deck
form.drivePrompt: Pick a Doc, Sheet, Slide, or file from your Google Drive.
form.driveHint: Docs work best as a bulleted outline — top bullet asks, indented bullet answers.

page.title: Convert your notes
page.subtitle: Drop a file and get a deck you don't have to fix — proper cloze, atomic cards, the right note types. Notion, PDF, Markdown, HTML, Word, and CSV too.
page.aiOffBody: You'll get rule-based cards from your file's structure.
page.upgradeClaude: Upgrade to write cards with Claude
page.signInToTurnOn: sign in to turn on AI
page.step1Body: PDF, Word, Notion export, Markdown, HTML, Excel, CSV, or PowerPoint. In Word docs, headings become card fronts; each bullet under a heading becomes its own card, and other text becomes the back. Images land on the back, and strikethrough text on a card becomes an Anki tag.
page.step2Body: Images, code blocks, cloze, and formatting carry over. Usually a few seconds.
page.step3Body: Your .apkg downloads automatically. Import it into Anki or AnkiDroid to start studying.

explore.heading: Make better decks
explore.mcqBody: Turn on MCQ and your next conversion includes multiple-choice cards alongside standard ones.
explore.photoBody: Snap a textbook page, lecture slide, or handwritten notes — 2anki builds the cards from the image.
explore.deckDefaultsBody: Set the deck name, card style (cloze or Q&A), card size (Short, Medium, Detailed), and AI options before you convert.

onboarding.step1Title: Drop a file, or pick a Notion page.
onboarding.step2Hint: Rename the deck, choose a card template, and set conversion options in Card options.
onboarding.step3Title: Convert your file into a deck.
onboarding.step3Hint: Press Convert — 2anki builds your .apkg in seconds.
onboarding.step4Title: Download the deck, or send it to AnkiWeb.
onboarding.step4Hint: Your deck downloads automatically. Import it in Anki or sync via AnkiWeb.

upload.overSplit: Many card fronts are just one or two words, which usually means the file split mid-sentence. Look over the deck before you study, or upload the original Word or Notion file for cleaner cards.

login.title: Log in to 2anki
login.subtitle: Turn your notes into Anki cards.
login.magicLinkHelp: We'll email you a sign-in link — no password needed.
login.magicLinkSuppressed: We can't email this address — it bounced or was unsubscribed earlier. Contact support@2anki.net to restore it, or use your password below.
login.magicLinkError: We couldn't send your sign-in link right now. Try again in a minute, or use your password below.

register.accountExists: This email already has an account — log in or reset your password.
register.title: Create account
register.errorGeneric: Something went wrong on our end. Try again, or email support@2anki.net if it keeps happening.
register.errorCreate: Couldn't create your account. If you already have one, log in instead.

forgot.title: Reset your password
forgot.description: Enter your email to receive a password reset link.

newPassword.title: Set a new password
newPassword.description: Choose a new password for your account.
newPassword.resetError: Could not reset your password. The link may have expired — request a new one.

checkEmail.title: Check your email
checkEmail.loginDescBefore: A login link was sent to
checkEmail.loginDescAfter: . Click the link to log in. It expires in 15 minutes.
checkEmail.resetDescBefore: A password reset link was sent to
checkEmail.resetDescAfter: . Click the link to reset your password. It expires in 15 minutes.
checkEmail.didntGetIt: Didn't get it? Check your spam folder, or
checkEmail.resendError: Couldn't send another link right now. Check your inbox and spam, or email support@2anki.net.
checkEmail.stillNothingPrefix: Still nothing after checking spam? Email

topMessage.emailVerified: Email verified. Sign in to continue.
topMessage.signInFallback: Couldn't sign you in. Try again, or use your email below.
topMessage.googleFailed: Couldn't sign you in with Google. Try again, or use your email below.
topMessage.microsoftFailed: Couldn't sign you in with Microsoft. Try again, or use your email below.
topMessage.notionCancelled: Couldn't sign you in with Notion. Try again, or use your email below.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.contextBanner: You're on the free plan — 100 cards per month.
pricing.introUS: Built for spaced repetition — MCAT, USMLE, bar exam, and language prep. Turn Notion pages, PDFs, and photos into Anki decks with AI — no account needed to start.
pricing.introDefault: Turn Notion pages, PDFs, and photos into Anki decks with AI — no account needed to start. Sign up free for 100 cards per month, then pay once by the day or week.
pricing.pricesNote: Prices in USD. Your card is charged in your local currency at checkout.
pricing.reassuranceOwn: Your decks are yours — native .apkg, works in any Anki client
pricing.educatorsBody: Making decks for a class, a tutoring group, or a course you sell? We're exploring tools for people who make decks for others.
pricing.philosophy: Free works forever. Paid plans support 2anki.net.

pass.everyFormat: Every file format — PDF, Word, EPUB, and more
pass.nativeApkg: Native .apkg — works in any Anki client

unlimited.benefitApkg: Native .apkg — works in any Anki client
unlimited.termsYearly: {{annualTotal}}/year, renews yearly. Cancel anytime — keeps access through the year you paid for.
unlimited.checkoutError: Couldn't start checkout. Try again, or email support@2anki.net.

features.heading: Everything 2anki does
features.subheading: Every plan includes all of it, free included. Paid plans lift the limits.
features.notionToAnkiDesc: Convert Notion pages and exports into decks
features.aiChatDesc: Draft and refine cards with Claude
features.photoDesc: Snap a page or slide — AI turns it into cards
features.mcqDesc: Quiz-style cards with up to 7 options
features.mindMapsDesc: Turn a page into a visual mind map
features.noteTypesDesc: Basic, Cloze, and your own card templates
features.shareDesc: Share a deck with a link
features.formatsDesc: PDF, Word, PowerPoint, EPUB, CSV, Markdown, and more

downloads.title: My decks
downloads.subtitle: Clean decks — proper cloze, atomic cards, no empty backs. Download straight into Anki.

empty.title: No decks yet
empty.body: Paste a Notion link or upload a file to make your first deck.

success.helper: No empty backs, no stray characters. Ready for Anki.

conversionResult.notionExpired: Notion connection expired. Reconnect to keep converting pages.
conversionResult.emptyDeckTeaching: 2anki makes a card from every Notion toggle — the toggle title becomes the question, what's inside becomes the answer. Wrap your key terms in toggles, then convert again.
conversionResult.ambiguousColumns: This database has more than two columns. Pick which column should be the front and back of each card.

paywall.headlineReached: You've reached your {{limit}} free cards this month
paywall.headlineUsed: You've used {{cardsUsed}} of your {{limit}} free cards this month
paywall.bodyNoReset: This conversion would go past your free limit, so it didn't finish. Get a pass to keep converting now.
paywall.bodyWithReset: This conversion would go past your free limit, so it didn't finish. Your free cards refresh on {{resetDate}}, or get a pass to keep converting now.

consent.title: Chat sends your messages to Anthropic
consent.body: Your messages and any files you attach go to Anthropic to generate replies. They aren't used to train models. 20 messages a month on the free plan.

columnMapping.title: Map your columns
columnMapping.differentColumns: Front and back must be different columns.

producer.title: Tell us what you need
producer.lead: We're exploring tools for people who make Anki decks for others. There's nothing to buy yet — answer two questions and we'll come to you first if we build it.
producer.purposeLabel: What are you making decks for?
producer.purposePlaceholder: e.g. MCAT tutoring, a Spanish course I sell, my biology class
producer.teamLabel: How many people will use them?
producer.thanksBody: Thanks. There's no teams product yet — we're still deciding whether to build it. If we do, you'll be among the first to try it.
producer.errSubmit: Couldn't save that — check your connection and try again.

cardOptions.resetError: Couldn't reset card options. Try again.

prereq.cloze: Turn on Cloze deletion cards first.

saveBar.saveError: Couldn't save your defaults. Try again.

deck.nameHint: Customize the deck name. Leave it empty if you use subpages.
deck.iconHint: Whether to include the Notion page icon and where to place it.
deck.toggleModeHint: Toggle header = card front; contents = card back. Nested toggles become their own cards. Open expands nested contents; Close keeps them collapsed for step-by-step review.

codeBlocks.heading: Code blocks
codeBlocks.intro: Code from your notes keeps its colors in Anki. Pick the look.
codeBlocks.themeHint: Colors for code from your notes. Switches between light and dark to match your Anki theme.

cardSize.heading: Card size
cardSize.intro: AI conversion uses this to decide how much fits on each card.
cardSize.shortDesc: 1 fact per card, ~80 characters per answer. Best for vocabulary, dates, formulas.
cardSize.mediumDesc: 1–2 facts per card, ~160 characters per answer. Good default for most notes.
cardSize.detailedDesc: 3–4 facts per card, ~320 characters per answer. Better for tightly grouped concepts you want to review together.

mcq.heading: Multiple choice questions (MCQ)
mcq.introPrefix: Photo to deck and the AI chat generate MCQ when this is on. You can also write them yourself with the MCQ syntax — see the
mcq.readAloudHint: Pick a voice for each field. Anki will speak it on the card.
mcq.question: Question
mcq.noVoiceHint: If your Anki device has no installed voice for the picked language, the audio stays silent.

overlappingCloze.heading: Overlapping cloze
overlappingCloze.intro: Turn a list, poem, or quote into a set of cards that hide one line at a time. Best for ordered things you recite — steps, lyrics, or a passage you're learning by heart.
overlappingCloze.showAllDesc: each card hides one item; the rest stays visible as context.
overlappingCloze.windowedDesc: each card hides one item; only the lines just before and after stay visible.

gated.groupClozeHelp: When one Notion toggle holds several :: blanks, put them all on a single card and reveal them together. Off by default — each :: makes its own card.
gated.inlineCodeHelp: When a toggle's contents contain inline code, hide the code as a cloze and use the toggle header as the hint. Works only when Cloze deletion is on.

audio.heading: Audio
audio.intro: Two settings, opposite effects. One adds Anki's built-in voice to your cards. The other hides raw MP3 URLs your source may carry.
audio.readAloudHint: Adds Anki's on-device voice to each card. Japanese, Korean, and Chinese are detected automatically; everything else reads in English. No audio file is added to your deck.
audio.pickVoiceHint: Choose a language and which side Anki reads. A pick here takes precedence over the automatic detection above.
audio.noVoiceHint: If your Anki device has no installed voice for the picked language, the audio stays silent.
audio.removeMp3: Remove MP3 links from audio files
audio.removeMp3Hint: Hides raw MP3 URLs that appear as visible text on cards. Embedded audio still plays — only the visible link is stripped.

templates.heading: Templates
templates.introPrefix: Pick the look of your generated cards and the names 2anki gives the Anki note types it creates. Saved note types from
templates.cardStyleHint: Pick the visual style applied during Notion → Anki conversion. Choose 'My note types' to use templates you saved in the Note types editor.
templates.basicNoteHint: Which of your saved note types to use for Basic (front/back) cards in this conversion.
templates.clozeNoteHint: Which of your saved note types to use for Cloze deletion cards in this conversion.
templates.inputNoteHint: Which of your saved note types to use for Type-the-answer cards in this conversion.
templates.basicTemplateHint: Name 2anki will give the Basic note type in Anki. Leave blank to use n2a-basic.
templates.clozeTemplateHint: Name 2anki will give the Cloze note type in Anki. Leave blank to use n2a-cloze.
templates.inputTemplateHint: Name 2anki will give the Type-the-answer note type in Anki. Leave blank to use n2a-input.
